scope: Manual encoding: pure relational-algebra variant differences are proved with bare sql_equiv; data-dependent ones are proved under an explicit hypothesis (FD/bijection/predicate-agreement) via the constraint layer; NULL/rounding/top-N-order differences are out_of_scope.
note: MERGED record: previously-judged instances are preserved (some may not have a .lean file on disk right now — see on_disk); new on-disk files are appended. Counts = prior + new incomers.

outcome_legend.pass: bare sql_equiv closes the pure-algebra difference.
outcome_legend.pass_under_hypothesis: true only under a stated data hypothesis (FD / bijection / predicate-agreement); proved with that as a premise.
outcome_legend.out_of_scope: needs NULL/rounding/top-N-order semantics we abstract away; no theorem.
outcome_legend.fail: a genuinely-algebraic goal sql_equiv could not close (none currently).

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: swapAppend + crossProduct_comm + join_comm + join_comm_image
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: join/cross-product commutativity via the dependent half-swap reindex; join_comm_image is the first-order 'swap operands then project' corollary.

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: restriction_join_left
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: selection pushdown into the left join input: sigma_{pL.left}(R join S) = (sigma_pL R) join S.

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: join_cond_congr
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: pointwise-equal join conditions give equal joins (closes ON a=b vs ON b=a).

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: union_idempotence + projection_union + select_union
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: R union R = R; projection and computed SELECT distribute over UNION.

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: relMax_union + relMin_union + relCount_eq_relCountDistinct_of_injOn
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: MAX/MIN over a union is the sup/inf of the two; COUNT(*) = COUNT(DISTINCT key) when key is injective on the rows.

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: FuncDepEq + cnt_pair_eq_of_FD + cnt_collapse_of_FD (@[simp]) + relCountDistinct_eq_of_factor
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: functional-dependency layer: GROUP BY (det,key) collapses to GROUP BY key under key->det; COUNT(DISTINCT) under a factoring bijection.

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: restriction_congr + cnt_eq_of_partition_eq + card_image_eq_of_fiber + select_congr
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: general congruences: restriction/select agree when predicates/row-maps agree on the data; same-partition => same group count / same distinct count.

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: SUBQUERY BRIDGES: mem_semijoin + in_subquery_eq_semijoin + semijoin_eq_join_image (+ combineTuple/splitTuple_combineTuple)
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: EXISTS/IN correlated subquery = semi-join; a semi-join is the DISTINCT left-projection of the inner join. The subquery<->join-form bridge (combineTuple is the named inverse of splitTuple).

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: crossProduct_assoc + assocAppend + combineTuple_splitTuple
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: three-way cross-product associativity up to the append re-bracketing (the data core of join associativity).

lemmas_added_to_toolbox.name: relCount_union_add_inter
lemmas_added_to_toolbox.statement: inclusion-exclusion for COUNT (|R|+|S| = |R∪S|+|R∩S|); UNION ALL = UNION on disjoint inputs (set model has no bag multiplicity).

planned_lemmas.name: full conditional join_assoc
planned_lemmas.why: lift crossProduct_assoc (done) through join conditions on the intermediate schema — needs a named assocAppend inverse + condition transport.

planned_lemmas.name: UNION ALL bag multiplicity, genuine top-N (ORDER BY LIMIT k)
planned_lemmas.why: out of the Finset set-model: top-N is identity here (correct for sets), bag multiplicity needs a different model.

instances.difference: the VARIABLE_GROUP filter is written as a SQL IN (…) list (baseline-sg) vs. an explicit OR-chain of equalities (probing). Everything else (join, GROUP BY DATE, SUM, ORDER BY) is identical, so the whole-query equivalence follows by congruence from the equality of the two WHERE predicates — proved her

probing vs baseline-sg. They are identical (same SELECT r.name, same JOIN, same GROUP BY r.name, r.length, same LIMIT 1) except for the ORDER BY key: CAST(COUNT() AS FLOAT) / CAST(r."length" AS FLOAT) vs COUNT() / r."length". Under set semantics ORDER BY (and LIMIT) is identity on the row-set, so th

semantic-probing vs baseline-sg are identical except the ORDER BY: ORDER BY SEA_BACKWARD_CITATIONS DESC vs ORDER BY SEA_BACKWARD_CITATIONS DESC, p.publication_number DESC (an extra tiebreak key). Everything else (the LEFT JOIN LATERAL FLATTEN, WHERE grant_date BETWEEN 20100101 AND 20181231, GROUP BY

semantic-probing writes the recovery rate as SUM(recovered) / SUM(confirmed) 100 (divide then multiply); probing writes SUM(recovered) 100.0 / SUM(confirmed) (multiply then divide). All four wrap it in ROUND(_,2); GROUP BY country, HAVING SUM(confirmed)>50000, ORDER BY … LIMIT 3 are shared.

(data functional dependency). All four variants use the same join order (BIRTH_DEATH_GROWTH_RATES b JOIN COUNTRY_NAMES_AREA); the only real difference is whether the output country_name is read from b (BIRTH_DEATH…) or from c/a (COUNTRY_NAMES_AREA). Those agree only when country_code → country_name
instances.note: Now PROVED under the cross-table FD: ON forces matching country_code on each joined row, FD then forces matching country_name, so SELECT b.country_name ≡ SELECT c.country_name (select_congr).

instances.difference: the dataset's own variants split two ways: - semantic-probing vs baseline-sg differ ONLY in output alias/quoting (YEAR_TOTAL vs "YEAR_TOTAL", year vs "year") — presentation-only; identical row-sets. - baseline-1 DROPS the major_category = 'Theft and Handling' conjunct — equal only if every 'Other Th

(needs a data hypothesis). All three variants share the same JOIN, WHERE reputation > 10, and ORDER BY COUNT() DESC LIMIT 1. The substantive difference is the GROUP BY granularity: - semantic-probing: GROUP BY display_name - probing / baseline-1: GROUP BY id, display_name
instances.note: Now PROVED under the FD hypothesis via cnt_pair_eq_of_FD (Constraints.lean): GROUP BY display_name and GROUP BY id,display_name give equal per-group COUNT(*).

instances.difference: both variants compute the per-category education-share as SUM(CASE WHEN LOWER(body) LIKE '%education%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) over COUNT(); they differ only in the ratio order — x 100 / total (semantic-probing) vs 100 x / total (baseline-sg). Across the four variants there are three independent differences: - the late-order ratio order: SUM(CASE…) 100 / COUNT() vs 100 SUM(CASE…) / COUNT() — pure algebra ( commutes), the pair proved here; - semantic-probing adds shippeddate IS NOT NULL to the predi

each preference tally is SUM(CASE WHEN PreferenceSeq=k THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) (semantic-probing) vs COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN PreferenceSeq=k THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) (baseline-sg). Everything else (the LEFT JOIN, GROUP BY StyleID,StyleName, ORDER BY) is identical.
